reviews.review_description: One star for the game, because you can't go any lower, plus one star for providing a demo so I could hate this game for free.
It's a great game especially, for those who absolutely love a good fantasy-action game.

I really very much felt like I was in a Legend of Zelda-style game in VR. What else could you ask for! Although I haven't purchased the full game yet, it is on my wish list and I will buy it at some point for sure!

Without that demo I would probably not have bought the game… Now I think I would have missed something great! 
A word to the wise: any studio making good stuff could benefit from publishing demos...

This is a really fun and creative demo that definitely makes you want more! Offering creative yet familiar gameplay and easy to understand instructions.

I almost fell over one time by moving and rotating too fast :P but cool thing is you can adjust those settings to make things as comfortable for you as you'd like them, among other vr options.

Definitely worth playing through and checking out the full version!

reviews.review_title: Seems to be a vanishing realms clone
reviews.review_description: or some such. muted graphics. wait a sale I reckon.
I like the demo but not everything was explained in the tutorial.

reviews.review_title: Why is the full version not available?
reviews.review_description: Please for the love of all that is good tell me why this is off the quest store completely?
and the full game is even greater!

I saw a lot complain about locomotion. You can choose the comfort mode, and yes, moving with stick will make you motion sickness, but I somehow overcome it easily. Enjoying the game, really cool. Graphics 10/10, Control 9/10, Gameplay 9/10, Sound 8/10.
Ever wanted to be IN a Zelda game? This is such a beautiful game world, and the Demo does not do it justice at all. I hope we see DLC, Expansions or a Sequel too.

But, as with other Oculus games, I get totally lost and there never seems to be an easily accessible settings or help menu.  I bumbled through in Journey until I got told to switch into my God [something] and had absolutely no clue how to do it.  None of the controls on either controller did much of anything, so after a few minutes I got frustrated and just quit.  That seems to be a pretty common thing with these games.  I never know what control does what and there's no easy way (that I can find) to find out, so I get frustrated and quit.  Seems a shame, but the Quest is really just a movie viewer for me the vast vast majority of the time.
